details: Compound 12a quote 'Orange yellow crystals; yield 0.83 g (43 %); M.p. 285-286 o C' verbatim present at experimental section of Molecules 8(4) 363-373 (10.3390/80400363, PMC6147013).
Glycoside 3c with mp 193.8-195.8 °C verbatim present in wang_2026_glucosamine_glycosides paper (md24020084); compound name and value confirmed.
Compound 11b 1,2-Bis(...ethyl...)disulfide with mp 118-120 °C verbatim in PMC6264341 experimental section; compound name (11b ethyl-linker) matches the chosen disulfide.
LiNO3·3H2O 30 °C is in Table 1 of chen_2024 hydrated salt paper (10.3390/nano14131077); table column 'Melting Temperature (°C)'; literature value cited from ref [43] — measured.

Compound 11 (methyl 4-(((1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-yl)oxy)methyl)-3-fluorobenzoate) appears with 'White solid (139.8 mg, 79%). Rf = 0.12 (CH2Cl2), m.p. 165-168 °C' verbatim in PMC11206691 (Molecules 29(12) 2942).
Compound 2f (3-(2-furanyl)-6-(3-chlorophenyl)-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole) Yield 66%; M.p.207-209°C verbatim in PMC6146422 (Molecules 7(8) 681).
Compound 2i (3-(2-furanyl)-6-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole) Yield 49%; M.p. 273-275°C verbatim in PMC6146422 (same paper as row 945).
2,2,4-trimethylpentane appears in Table 1.2 'Experimental melting points for some octanes' with -107 °C in 2005 Yalkowsky dissertation; legacy identifier OK; measured/experimental column confirmed.
2,6-dime-4-heptanol row 54 in Table 5 'BP (Exp.) 178.0' in PMC3127127 (Int J Mol Sci 12 2448); abbreviated name in table; measured column confirmed.

Compound 14 6-Methyl-1-(4-methylbenzyl)... 'as a white solid (300 mg, 75.75%), mp = 230-232 °C' verbatim in PMC12984852 (ijms27052306).
Compound 5a 5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-4-...pyridazin-3(2H)-one with 'mp = 106-107 °C (EtOH)' verbatim in PMC12943719.
Compound 12h with 'Cream solid; yield: 68%; MP = 212-214 °C' verbatim in PMC13093879 (d5ra09311b RSC Adv); compound name matches the bis-triazole bis-amide ether structure of 12h.

The evidence_quote 'Dark red solid;' is verbatim in 2019 Rubtsov paper but does not contain the mp value; the full sentence is 'Dark red solid; 1.64 g, 86 % yield; m.p. 168-170 °С (toluene).' The quote is truncated and omits the measurement — fails Step 4 (quote must support the value).

Paper 090 (PMC6146422, 10.3390/70800681) compound 2k: 'Yield 62 %; M.p.115-117°C' — quote 'M.p.115-117°C' verbatim, compound 3-(2-furanyl)-6-phenyloxymethyl-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole matches.

Paper liu_2024_matrine_salts_thermal (10.3390/molecules29030567) Section 2 results: '102.90 °C and 140.90 °C, which are between' — quote verbatim; 140.90 °C is the second endothermic peak of MAT-SAL-H2O salt monohydrate. Note: source label says 'Pharmaceuticals' but DOI is in Molecules; DOI identifier matches paper, Q6 passes.
Paper 113 (PMC13103808, 10.1021/acsomega.6c01037) section 2.3.1 for compound 5: 'to afford 6.2 g of white crystals (89%) of 5 , mp 55–56 °C' — quote verbatim, compound matches.

Paper khan_2017_piroxicam_cocrystal Table 2 lists 'Piroxicam-sodium acetate ... 324 (coformer mp)' but the row's evidence_quote 'Piroxicam-sodium acetate ... Melting point coformer 324' is a paraphrase with ellipsis (...) joining tokens that are not adjacent in the paper; this is not a verbatim string. The value 324 °C and data_type=measured are otherwise correct.
